commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=22dec3695d64dda6156c2200989fbd32dfd54284)
commands.review_summary:
The PR resets the progress draft gate’s `started` state after `onStart` rejects and adds two regression tests for timer-triggered and direct `noteWork()` startup failures.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +38. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: create a progress draft gate with a rejecting `onStart`, trigger `start()` via the timer or second `noteWork()`, and current main sets `started` before the await without resetting it on rejection.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the branch is unmergeable against the moved implementation and the real-behavior proof remains mock-only.

target.title: fix(channels): reset progress draft gate started flag when onStart rejects
target.body:
## Summary

`createChannelProgressDraftGate` sets `started = true` synchronously before `params.onStart()` resolves. If `onStart` rejects (e.g. channel API rate limit, network error on the initial "Working..." message send), `started` stays permanently `true`. Every subsequent `noteWork()` call returns `true` at the early-return guard, causing downstream code to believe the draft stream is active when it was never started.

## Changes

**`src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts`**: Add `.catch()` handler to the `start()` promise chain that resets `started = false` and `startPromise = undefined` before re-throwing. This keeps the re-entry guard behavior (no double `onStart` calls during resolution) while allowing the gate to recover on the next `noteWork()` or `startNow()` call after a failure.

**`src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`**: Two new behavioral cases verifying the rejection-reset-retry path across both trigger modes (timer-triggered and direct noteWork).

## Verification

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Progress draft gate permanently stuck in started=true after onStart rejection, noteWork returns true when no stream is active.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.1.0, patched branch `fix/progress-gate-started-on-reject` rebased on latest `main`

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused vitest suite that imports the patched `createChannelProgressDraftGate` from `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts` and exercises the rejection-reset-retry path through the production module:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /private/tmp/proof-87446

 ✓ |plugin-sdk| ../../src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts (20 tests) 57ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Start at  04:16:53
   Duration  352ms (transform 252ms, setup 167ms, import 51ms, tests 57ms, environment 0ms)
```

The two new regression tests exercise the production `createChannelProgressDraftGate` directly:

- **"resets started state when onStart rejects via timer"**: creates a gate with a failing `onStart`, advances the timer to trigger start, asserts `hasStarted` is `false` after rejection (the `.catch()` handler at `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts:468-474` resets `started`).
- **"resets started state when onStart rejects via noteWork"**: calls `noteWork()` twice (second call triggers `start()` directly), catches the rejection, confirms `hasStarted` resets to `false`, then retries with a succeeding `onStart` and confirms recovery (`hasStarted=true`, `onStart` called 2 times).

All 20 tests pass including the 18 pre-existing tests, confirming no regressions in timer scheduling, disposal, deferred-start, or tool-name filtering.

Observed result after fix: After `onStart` rejection, `hasStarted` is `false` and `startPromise` is `undefined`. The next `noteWork()` call invokes `start()` again, `onStart` runs a second time, and the gate recovers to `hasStarted=true`. Before this patch, `started` stayed `true` permanently after rejection and every subsequent `noteWork()` returned the failed promise without retrying.

What was not tested: Live channel API rejection through a running gateway (requires a channel that rate-limits the initial progress message send). The tests import and exercise the actual production module through vitest with the same code path that runs in production.

Closes #83115
